objective: Establish dying magic through Hermione's daily experience — spell failures, research dead ends, institutional strain
opposition: The decay is systemic and her usual competence cannot solve it; every archive is exhausted
turn: She realizes the problem exceeds what conventional research can reach — the scale is larger than the Ministry admits
consequence_cost: Her professional identity is shaken; if she cannot solve this, she does not know what she is for

objective: The Malfoy library proposition arrives — the last uncatalogued major collection
opposition: Her history with the Manor, the trauma, the fact that accepting means returning to the place where she was tortured
turn: The lead is the only viable path; professional necessity overrides personal resistance
consequence_cost: She must enter the site of her worst memory and negotiate with the boy who watched

objective: Hermione decides to go to the Manor
opposition: Internal resistance, the scar she adjusts without thinking, a sleepless night
turn: Competence wins over fear — she frames it as work because that is how she survives
consequence_cost: She commits to proximity with her worst memories; the decision costs her a night's sleep

objective: Draco alone in the Manor — establish his isolation, the house too large, his careful precise life
opposition: The Manor's emptiness, rooms he avoids, the Dark Mark's new restless pain
turn: The Mark's behavior is worsening and he cannot ignore it — he needs help he cannot get from anyone willing to help
consequence_cost: He must accept help from someone whose presence in this house will cost him

objective: The negotiation — Hermione arrives, terms of access set
opposition: Their shared history, the formality masking everything underneath, last names as armor
turn: The terms are agreed: library for Mark help. Professional. Civil. But the air in the room knows better
consequence_cost: Both are now bound to proximity they did not choose; the professional frame is already under pressure

objective: Draco walks Hermione to the guest wing; the Manor opens a forgotten corridor
opposition: The house's behavior — a shortcut that should not exist — unsettles him
turn: He notices the Manor responding to her presence and chooses not to mention it
consequence_cost: The house is already changing; his silence about it is the first thing he withholds

objective: First Mark examination — Hermione's clinical approach to the Dark Mark on Draco's forearm
opposition: The intimacy of sustained physical contact; his rigidity; the Mark's unexpected responsiveness to her diagnostic spells
turn: The Mark behaves differently under her touch — the dark magic in it responds with something like recognition
consequence_cost: Clinical distance is harder to maintain than she planned; the Mark is not a standard Dark artifact

objective: Hermione begins organizing the library — the scale and chaos of the Malfoy collection
opposition: Centuries of uncatalogued acquisition, multiple languages, some texts resistant to cataloguing spells
turn: She builds a system and begins to find patterns — the collection is extraordinary and she is in her element
consequence_cost: The work will take weeks at minimum; she is committed to sustained presence

objective: Alone in the Manor at night, the house shifts — Hermione gets genuinely lost
opposition: The corridors rearrange; landmarks she used that morning are gone; twenty minutes of disorientation before the house releases her
turn: She reclassifies her situation: this is not a house with quirks, this is a sentient structure and she is inside it
consequence_cost: Her sense of control is compromised; she must account for the Manor as an active variable

objective: Draco observes the Manor treating Hermione differently — doors, shelves, warmth, the house's attention redirected
opposition: His lifelong familiarity with the house thrown off; the house prefers her in ways it has never preferred him
turn: He recognizes that the Manor is responding to Hermione specifically, not just to company
consequence_cost: His inheritance — the house, the name, the legacy — responds to an outsider more than to him

objective: Narcissa visits; she walks through the Manor with practiced ease, notices the shifts
opposition: Narcissa's careful non-answers; her recognition without surprise when she sees anomalous doors
turn: Narcissa confirms through reaction, not words, that this behavior is significant and has precedent
consequence_cost: Draco knows his mother is withholding something about the house; she will not be pushed

objective: Library research deepens — Hermione and Draco working together, intellectual sparring, the pleasure of collaboration
opposition: Their caution with each other; the residual formality that keeps breaking under the pressure of genuine engagement
turn: They find a rhythm — not warmth but the specific pleasure of two rigorous minds matched. He catches errors she misses; she finds connections he cannot see
consequence_cost: The intellectual intimacy is its own kind of proximity; they are enjoying each other and neither is ready to name it

objective: Hermione feels the Mark resonance at distance for the first time — his proximity, his pain, without touching him
opposition: The resonance is invasive, uninvited; she does not understand the mechanism; it blurs the boundary between her body and his
turn: She rationalizes it as diagnostic residue and decides not to tell him
consequence_cost: She is now carrying information about their connection that she is actively concealing

objective: Scene with Harry outside the Manor — friendship, warmth, the ordinary world
opposition: Harry's concern, her need to appear fine, the scar she adjusts twice without thinking
turn: Harry asks about sleep; she lies. The friendship is real but she is editing herself for him
consequence_cost: She is increasingly isolated by what she knows and what she is hiding — from Draco, from Harry, from herself

objective: Mark examination with deeper probing — the clinical frame under sustained pressure
opposition: His pulse under her fingers; the Mark quieting specifically under her touch; the intimacy they are both pretending is professional
turn: The Mark's responsiveness to her is now undeniable — the dark magic recognizes her in a way that exceeds diagnostic explanation
consequence_cost: The professional frame is a fiction they are maintaining by mutual agreement; the body knows what they will not say

objective: Discovery of the gated archive — a room that holds only when both are present
opposition: The room destabilizes when one leaves; access is conditional on proximity; they test this empirically
turn: The house is gating critical research behind their togetherness — treating their connection as a key
consequence_cost: They cannot access what they need independently; the Manor is forcing collaboration and proximity as the price of knowledge

objective: Narcissa and Hermione alone — the careful conversation about the Manor's history and nature
opposition: Narcissa's strategic partial truth; her refusal to be fully transparent; Hermione's insistence on directness meeting Narcissa's obliqueness
turn: Narcissa reveals enough to unsettle — the house was built to sustain a line and takes this mandate seriously — without naming the mechanism
consequence_cost: Hermione knows the house has precedent for this behavior, making the current situation less anomalous and more frightening

objective: Research progress — magical decay connected to war damage to ancient anchoring infrastructure
opposition: The texts are dense and archaic; the infrastructure they describe was built by Malfoys centuries ago; institutional knowledge was lost
turn: The decay has a cause: war damage to foundational magic. The Malfoys are uniquely positioned to understand it because they helped build it
consequence_cost: The research justification for her presence deepens — this is not optional work but critical to magical survival

objective: The Manor at night — Hermione alone, feeling the house settle and the Mark resonance telling her where Draco is
opposition: The invasive intimacy of knowing his location and wakefulness without consent or contact
turn: She lies in the house that knows her and feels the boy two floors up who does not know she feels him
consequence_cost: The connection is deepening beyond what she can attribute to professional artifact; she is running out of rationalizations

objective: Mark examination goes deeper — the curse memory floods, Draco relives taking the Mark at sixteen, Hermione feels it through the resonance
opposition: The memory is violent, involuntary, devastating — neither chose this exposure; the Mark decided
turn: Through the resonance, Hermione feels the fear, pain, and age of the boy who took the Mark. Draco is exposed in the worst possible way
consequence_cost: The clinical frame is permanently broken; she has been inside his worst moment; he cannot take that back

objective: Aftermath — the library, the silence, the inability to discuss what happened directly
opposition: The rawness of what just occurred; neither has language for it; the old defenses feel inadequate
turn: The silence between them changes — not careful distance but the quiet of shared pain. They sit together and it is enough
consequence_cost: Something irrevocable has occurred in the space between examiner and subject; they are no longer those things to each other

objective: Hermione's guard lowering — small domestic moment, unguarded interiority, the beginning of safety in the Manor
opposition: Her own resistance to feeling safe here; the awareness that safety in this house is a contradiction she cannot resolve
turn: She catches herself thinking about the shape of his hands during a meal. She is startled by her own wanting
consequence_cost: Her defenses are at their lowest point since arriving — she is feeling at home in the place where she was tortured

objective: The drawing room appears — she turns a corner and the room is there
opposition: The room itself: the chandelier, the marble, the cold. Trauma preserved in merciless sensory detail. Her body will not move
turn: The Manor has opened this room at the moment of maximum vulnerability — whether by design, by condition, or by something else
consequence_cost: She is inside her worst memory, physically, and the house either orchestrated this or let it happen

objective: Draco finds her; the reckoning — what each remembers, the unfair and inadequate things said, leaving separately
opposition: Their shared history at its worst; neither can handle this cleanly; honesty and cruelty are indistinguishable in this room
turn: They tell each other what they remember and it is ugly and real. She says unfair things. He says inadequate things. They leave separately
consequence_cost: The war is on the table between them. The drawing room cannot be pretended away. Civil distance is no longer available to either of them

objective: Post-drawing-room gentleness — the days after, careful and not cold, a different register
opposition: The residual rawness; neither knows how to be after what happened; the old modes do not fit
turn: They default into something new: gentler, more honest, as if the confrontation burned off a layer of performance
consequence_cost: A third register between them emerges — not professional, not traumatic, but domestic and quiet

objective: Library domesticity — meals together, books on chairs, tea made right, Draco cataloguing her expressions
opposition: His own resistance to what he is feeling; the habit of not-wanting that has organized his post-war life
turn: She laughs and he realizes he has been watching her for days. She sleeps in the library and he stays
consequence_cost: He is allowing himself to want something, which for him is the most dangerous possible development

objective: Theo Nott visits — Draco with a friend, different register, the wit sharper
opposition: Theo's pragmatism clashing with Draco's guarded optimism; Theo sees the situation immediately
turn: Theo says almost nothing about Hermione except one honest line alone with Draco afterward
consequence_cost: Draco is seen by someone who knows him — the situation is real enough to be observed by an outsider

objective: Mark resonance now invasive — mutual peripheral sensation during examination, Hermione flinches at the vulnerability
opposition: The connection has outgrown what either can control; during examination their awareness leaks into each other
turn: She flinches away from the mutual sensation. The resonance is no longer one-directional — he can feel her too
consequence_cost: The bond has escalated beyond passive awareness into something that violates the boundary of individual experience

objective: Hermione finds the bonding texts — the Manor as bonding artifact, the resonance as designed amplification
opposition: The texts are unambiguous; the mechanism matches their experience exactly; her feelings are now epistemically suspect
turn: She does not tell Draco. She tells herself she needs to verify. She knows this is a lie. She continues proximity while concealing
consequence_cost: The information asymmetry begins — every subsequent moment of connection occurs under knowledge she is withholding

objective: Draco confused by Hermione's withdrawal — he interprets retreat as rejection, retreats himself, the Manor goes cold
opposition: Her briskness, his self-blame, the corridors lengthening between them, the archive refusing to open
turn: He cannot understand the change and defaults to his oldest interpretation: he has been found wanting
consequence_cost: The mutual withdrawal feeds the Manor's coldness, which feeds the withdrawal — a degenerating loop

objective: The scar reveal — glamour flickers during examination, he sees MUDBLOOD carved in her arm, he touches it, she flinches, his hand stays
opposition: The scar's exposure after chapters of concealment; her vulnerability; his complicity in the event that created it
turn: The most intimate scene so far: no kiss, no grand gesture. He traces the letters. The silence. His honesty. Her devastating question
consequence_cost: The power dynamic shifts permanently — she is no longer the strong one helping the damaged one. They are both marked by the same house

objective: Ron appears — meeting in London, the specific texture of their post-divorce relationship
opposition: The ways they know each other too well; his worry that looks like concern but borders on the pain of watching her return to what hurt her
turn: He says something careful about the Manor. She does not know if he means the house or the heir
consequence_cost: The outside world has opinions about what she is doing; the people who love her are worried

objective: The magical decay is now public crisis — Floo failures, healing rationed, accidental magic in children
opposition: The institutional scale of the failure; the research she has not finished because she left the Manor
turn: The crisis has outgrown what she can solve alone or from outside the Manor; the work is urgent
consequence_cost: The research is not optional; returning to the Manor becomes professionally necessary as well as personally desired

objective: She returns to the Manor and the house opens with warmth
opposition: The ease of the welcome; the house treating her return as homecoming; her awareness that she is being seduced by architecture
turn: The Manor opens for her like recognition, and the comfort of that response is more disturbing than the house's hostility ever was
consequence_cost: She is choosing this. The house wants her back and she wants to be wanted back and she cannot separate the two

objective: Deeper archive opens — texts on original anchoring construction, decodable only through collaborative expertise
opposition: The texts require both his family knowledge and her theoretical training; neither can read them alone
turn: Working together, they decode material no one has read in centuries — the intellectual partnership at its height
consequence_cost: The work confirms their indispensability to each other; the collaboration is also the intimacy

objective: The Manor at its most seductive — warm, golden, stable; Draco stops pretending he is not watching her
opposition: His resistance, now nearly gone; the house creating conditions where togetherness feels effortless
turn: He lets himself want this — not just her but the whole of it: the work, the house, the life where someone is here
consequence_cost: He has dropped his last defense; if this fails, there is no position to fall back to

objective: Building to the kiss — library at night, tired, close over a text, the distance collapsing
opposition: Their caution, the doubt about the Manor, the fear that the first move will break what they have built
turn: He says something dry about a translation. She looks at him. The distance is simply gone. Or she closes it. Or he does
consequence_cost: They are touching each other with intent for the first time; the terror and the want are the same thing

objective: The first kiss and its aftermath — specific, embodied, not a fade-to-black; the doubt present in her body during and after
opposition: The Manor's possible influence; the resonance between them amplified; her inability to separate real desire from engineered response
turn: The kiss is real and desperate and she cannot verify that it is hers. Afterward, alone, the Manor is the warmest it has been and she cannot trust the warmth
consequence_cost: The physical threshold is crossed but the doubt is worse, not better — desire and suspicion coexist in every nerve

objective: Draco in the archive alone — the room holds briefly, unstable; he finds the bonding texts
opposition: The texts are unambiguous; the mechanism matches; the dates of her withdrawal align with when she must have found them
turn: He realizes she has known for weeks — the withdrawal, the return, the kiss all occurred under knowledge she concealed
consequence_cost: Every moment of their connection is retroactively contaminated by the secret she kept about it

objective: The confrontation — specific, brutal, about the information asymmetry not the bond itself
opposition: Her defense is real but insufficient; his anger is about trust and agency, not about the bond; neither can hear the other
turn: She stayed. She kissed him. She treated their connection as something to study before sharing. His worst fear confirmed: even this was done to him
consequence_cost: Their defenses are both fully deployed and both devastating — his isolation, her rationalization

objective: She leaves the Manor
opposition: The departure; the house responding — corridors contracting, temperature dropping, the architecture grieving or withdrawing
turn: She walks out. The house goes cold behind her
consequence_cost: The relationship is broken. The research is unfinished. The Manor is inert. All three losses are concrete

objective: Hermione in her flat — ordinary silence, no shifting walls, no resonance, the gutted absence of the Manor
opposition: The flatness of her ordinary life; the Mark resonance degraded by distance but not gone — she can feel him hurting
turn: She is alone in a way that the Manor made her forget she was. The ordinary walls do not respond to her
consequence_cost: The separation renders the Manor's influence legible by contrast — its absence is its own kind of evidence

objective: The magical decay accelerates — Floo down for hours, institutional crisis, the research undone
opposition: The crisis does not pause for their personal rupture; the work is urgent and she is the person most capable of finishing it
turn: The world needs what she and Draco were doing together, regardless of what it cost them personally
consequence_cost: Professional necessity and personal need are converging toward the same demand: return

objective: Harry says the hard true thing; she begins to understand what she actually did and why
opposition: Harry's honesty; her resistance to seeing her concealment clearly; the gap between her rationalization and her actual fear
turn: She was not protecting him by withholding — she was protecting herself from having to decide before she was ready
consequence_cost: The self-narrative cracks; she can see her own agency in the rupture, which is the precondition for choosing differently

objective: Draco alone in the cold Manor — the house inert, the Mark at its worst, corridors sealed
opposition: The Manor without the bond; his isolation now worse than before she came because he knows what it felt like to not be alone
turn: The house is punishing him or grieving or simply inert without the resonance to animate it — he cannot tell which
consequence_cost: He is where he started but worse, because now he knows what he is missing

objective: Narcissa comes — the revelation about the Manor's bonding magic: it cannot fabricate, only amplify
opposition: Narcissa's own history with the house — Lucius never resonated like this; her revelation is also her grief
turn: The doubt is reframed: not is this real but how much of this is ours. Narcissa tells him Lucius never resonated with the house the way he does with Hermione
consequence_cost: The question changes shape. Fabrication vs amplification is a different problem than real vs false — and the second problem is survivable

objective: The return — she comes back or he goes to her; the drawing room entered together; the first-name shift; hopeful, unresolved ending
opposition: The doubt persists; the damage is real; the research is unfinished; nothing is clean
turn: They choose each other despite the unresolved doubt. The drawing room is entered by mutual choice. The first names land quietly
consequence_cost: Book 1 closes: the architecture is involuntary but the choice to inhabit it is not. The magic is still dying, the bond is still uncertain, they are still choosing
